method: RepoPrompt architecture + infrastructure analysis (no LLM call)

findings.title: Router-to-resource mapping is ambiguous
findings.evidence: main.py includes resources, additives, essential_oils, colorants, inci, calculate, auth. But resources.py ALSO owns /additives, creating a second owner. The domain split is halfway between 'resource discovery bundle' and 'per-domain modules'.
findings.recommendation: Pick a single axis. Recommended: per-domain routers (one file per top-level noun). Delete resources.py, keep additives.py/essential_oils.py/colorants.py, add oils.py. This also fixes CQ-01.
findings.payoff: Clear mental model for contributors; kills a class of bugs.

findings.title: Services return ad-hoc dicts and ORM-style objects inconsistently
findings.evidence: quality_metrics_calculator returns a QualityMetrics class (custom __init__ of **metrics). fatty_acid_calculator returns a FattyAcidProfile class. lye_calculator returns LyeResult. But three_format_inci_generator returns a 4-tuple of Optional[str,str,str,List[Dict]]. label_generator returns (str, List[InciIngredientDetail]).
findings.recommendation: Standardize on Pydantic models (not hand-rolled classes) as service outputs. Or use @dataclass(frozen=True) consistently. The tuple returns in INCI services are the worst offenders — caller has to remember positional meaning.
findings.payoff: Type checker catches misuse; swagger/responses line up with service layer; reduces coupling between routers and service internals.

findings.title: No request/service boundary for the business rules
findings.evidence: calculate.py reaches directly into DB (select(Oil).where...) then hands IDs into services. Services accept dicts, classes, and floats. Business rules (e.g., 'lye % must sum to 100', 'superfat warnings') are half in Pydantic validators, half in validation.py, half in service code.
findings.recommendation: Introduce a `CalculationInput` domain object built from the HTTP request + DB-loaded oils/additives. Services accept this object only. Router stays thin: parse → load refs → build input → call service → persist → serialize.
findings.payoff: Enables a headless calculation library (future CLI or desktop client). Makes calculation unit-testable without FastAPI.

findings.title: Two DB URLs (async + sync) both required at startup
findings.evidence: config.py forces DATABASE_URL and DATABASE_URL_SYNC. Only Alembic env.py and some scripts use the sync URL.
findings.recommendation: Derive sync URL from async URL (`settings.DATABASE_URL.replace('+asyncpg', '')`) in a @property. Removes one required env var and one source of drift (they can desync).
findings.payoff: One less env var to document, copy, and vault.

findings.title: Engine has no pooling or health-check config
findings.evidence: app/db/base.py: create_async_engine(settings.DATABASE_URL, echo=settings.DEBUG, future=True). No pool_size, max_overflow, pool_pre_ping, or connect_args.
findings.recommendation: Add pool_pre_ping=True (handles Postgres restarts gracefully under the 4-worker uvicorn setup) and explicit pool_size/max_overflow tuned to worker count. Set statement_timeout via connect_args for safety.
findings.payoff: Survives Postgres bounces; bounds resource usage per worker.

findings.title: Ingestion scripts diverge in DB-access style
findings.evidence: scripts/import_oils.py uses raw psycopg2. scripts/import_oils_database.py, seed_database.py, test_seed_idempotent.py use async SQLAlchemy. Two styles for the same job.
findings.recommendation: Kill the psycopg2 path. All import/seed scripts should use the async session factory via a small `scripts/_db.py` helper. Fewer dependencies, one code path, no raw SQL injection surface.
findings.payoff: Remove psycopg2-binary from hot path (keep only for Alembic), fewer security review targets.

findings.title: Seeding is a 547-line script instead of idempotent SQL + YAML fixtures
findings.evidence: scripts/seed_data.py (547 lines) contains hardcoded oil/additive definitions in Python. Every new ingredient requires a code change and a redeploy.
findings.recommendation: Move seed data to YAML/JSON fixtures under data/seeds/ (you already have data/oils/saponified_inci.json — extend the pattern). Seeder reads fixtures, upserts by unique key. Then 'add new oil' is a data PR, not a code PR.
findings.payoff: Non-engineers (Shale/Jackie, the actual domain experts) can submit data updates. Faster iteration on reference data.

findings.title: Migration numbering is inconsistent
findings.evidence: migrations/versions has both sequential names (001_initial_schema.py..007) and hash-prefixed names (21ba28925bc0, aeff6055c7b3, b76343b095ad, bc8a5b9f9392, d4e242f8056c). Plus the stray \$(ls -t directory.
findings.recommendation: Pick one: either all sequential-numbered (human-friendly, what Alembic defaults to discourages, but common), or all hash-prefixed (Alembic default). Mixing signals the team is fighting the tool.
findings.payoff: Easier to reason about migration history; matches team workflow.

findings.title: CORS config is a comma-separated string parsed at runtime
findings.evidence: config.py has ALLOWED_ORIGINS as a single string, split on comma in allowed_origins_list property. Pydantic-settings supports List[str] natively via JSON or delimiter.
findings.recommendation: Use Pydantic's field validator to parse on load. Validate each entry is a valid URL. Default to [] in prod (fail-closed), not to a dev-localhost list (current .env.example leaks that default).
findings.payoff: Fail-closed CORS; catches typos at startup.

findings.title: Two deployment paths — docker-compose and Ansible+Quadlet — without clear lane separation
findings.evidence: docker-compose.yml + docker-compose.prod.yml + podman/systemd/*.container + ansible/roles/. Three ways to bring up the stack.
findings.recommendation: Scope each: docker-compose.yml = dev only (no prod env file), Quadlet + Ansible = prod only. Delete docker-compose.prod.yml if Ansible is authoritative, or delete the compose files if not used locally.
findings.payoff: One source of truth per environment. Less drift between dev and prod.

findings.title: claudedocs/, agent-responses/, working/ are inside the repo
findings.evidence: 100+ agent-response files, claudedocs/ reports, working/ scratchpads committed. These are AI session artifacts, not product code.
findings.recommendation: Move to a git-ignored or sibling-repo path (e.g., .agent-workspace/ gitignored). Keep only genuinely useful reports as docs/adr/ entries. Current mix pollutes git history and grep results.
findings.payoff: Faster grep, cleaner diffs, clearer repo boundary between product and process.

findings.title: No structured logging
findings.evidence: Codebase has zero logging setup beyond FastAPI/uvicorn defaults. Errors that get caught (CQ-07) just return without logging context.
findings.recommendation: Add structlog or loguru with JSON output, correlate via a request-id middleware. Then broad except-clauses can at least log with structure before returning.
findings.payoff: Production triage becomes possible. Pairs with CQ-07.

findings.title: Spec directory sprawl (.specify/, agent-os/, specs/)
findings.evidence: Three parallel spec directories, some features spec'd in two places (e.g., 002-lye-purity in .specify/specs and specs/).
findings.recommendation: Already noted in CLAUDE.md. Pick one canonical location going forward. Archive the losers to docs/archive/specs/.
findings.payoff: New contributors stop asking 'which spec is current?'
